description: Species code matching fresh's parameters_habitat_thresholds.csv (BT, CH, CO, ST, WCT, SK, KO, PK, CM, RB, CT, DV, GR). Row key.
Does the species spawn in lakes? When yes, lnk_rules_build emits a `waterbody_type: L` rule under spawn:.
Does the species spawn in streams? When yes, lnk_rules_build emits the stream-edge rule (edge_types_explicit: [1000, 1100, 2000, 2300]) plus the river polygon rule (waterbody_type: R) under spawn:.

Should the spawn-stream rule fire on segments INSIDE a waterbody polygon (waterbody_key non-null)? `no` emits `in_waterbody: false` so the stream rule matches only outside-polygon segments - strict partition that pairs with the polygon rules. `yes` (or absent) omits the field; rule matches both inside and outside polygons.

Does the species rear in lakes? When yes (and not rear_lake_only / rear_no_fw), lnk_rules_build emits a `waterbody_type: L` rule with edge_types_explicit: [1000, 1100] mainlines filter + lake_ha_min from rear_lake_ha_min or fresh thresholds.
emits: waterbody_type: L + edge filter (rear, additive branch)

description: Override: this species ONLY rears in lakes. When yes, lnk_rules_build emits a single `waterbody_type: L` rule under rear:, no edge filter, no other rules. Used by SK, KO. lake_ha_min still honoured.

When yes, emits `area_only: true` on the L polygon rule. fresh derives the lake_rearing bucket flag but excludes the rule from the main rear predicate. Linear `rearing_km` excludes polygon segments; `lake_rearing_ha` still rolls up. Only honoured in the additive rear branch - rear_lake_only ignores this column (the L rule there IS the rear classification).
Override: species has no freshwater rearing (CM, PK). When yes, all rear rules are dropped. Higher precedence than rear_lake / rear_stream / etc.
Does the species rear in streams? When yes (and not rear_lake_only / rear_no_fw), lnk_rules_build emits the stream-edge rule + the river polygon rule under rear:.

Should the rear-stream rule fire on segments INSIDE a waterbody polygon? Same shape as spawn_stream_in_waterbody. `no` emits `in_waterbody: false` (strict partition matching bcfishpass's per-species access SQL). `yes` (or absent) is permissive - counts polygon-mainlines as rearing.

Does the species rear in wetland-flow segments? When yes, lnk_rules_build emits the 1050/1150 stream-flow carve-out (edge_types_explicit: [1050, 1150], thresholds: false) under rear:. The W polygon rule is gated separately by rear_wetland_polygon.

Should lnk_rules_build emit the `waterbody_type: W` polygon rule (drives wetland_rearing flag, populating wetland_rearing_ha rollup)? When yes (requires rear_wetland=yes), emits the W rule with edge_types_explicit: [1000, 1100] mainlines filter. When no (or absent), only the carve-out emits.
When yes, emits `area_only: true` on the W polygon rule. Same shape as rear_lake_area_only.

Override: drop the edge_types filter on the rear-stream rule (matches every accessible segment regardless of edge_type). Used by BT in bcfishpass bundle to match bcfishpass's BT rear access logic. Higher precedence than rear_stream's normal emission.

Should the river polygon rule (`waterbody_type: R`) skip the cw_min check? When yes, emits `channel_width: [0, 9999]` on the river rule - biology of large rivers spawning/rearing fish regardless of FWA cw measurement. Spawn-side bypass; complements the rearing stream_order bypass (rear_stream_order_bypass).

Currently inert: column reads, no SQL emission yet. Once fresh#158 ships frs_order_child, lnk will emit a per-species frs_order_child invocation when this is yes. Captures the bcfishpass rearing bypass `(stream_order_parent >= 5 AND stream_order = 1)`.

Habitat type that spawn rules' segments must be connected to (e.g. "rearing" for SK / KO - spawning happens within reach of rearing lakes). When set, every emitted spawn rule carries `requires_connected: <value>`.
Max network distance (metres) between a spawn segment and the connected target. When set with spawn_requires_connected, every spawn rule carries `connected_distance_max: <value>`.
Direction to walk from spawn segment when looking for the connected target. Drives the spawn_connected: block in rules.yaml.
Bridge gradient cap when tracing toward the connected target. Drives spawn_connected.gradient_max.
Channel width minimum on the trace to the connected target. Drives spawn_connected.channel_width_min.
Semicolon-separated list of edge_type integers permitted on the trace toward the connected target. Drives spawn_connected.edge_types.
Same shape as spawn_requires_connected, applied to every emitted rear rule.
Same shape as spawn_connected_distance_max, applied to rear rules.
Per-species override for the L polygon rule's lake_ha_min. When set, wins over fresh's parameters_habitat_thresholds.csv value. Honoured in both rear_lake_only and additive rear branches.
Per-species override for the W polygon rule's wetland_ha_min. fresh has no wetland threshold column so this is the only source.
Free-form notes per species - biology references, citations, NGE-vs-bcfishpass deltas, TODO lifelines. Not consumed by lnk_rules_build.
